title: Thai Larb Lettuce Cups
description: Spicy ground chicken tossed with lime, fish sauce, and fresh herbs, served in crisp butter lettuce cups.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground chicken, breaking it into small pieces, and cook until no longer pink, about 6-8 minutes.
Remove from heat and toss with lime juice, fish sauce, and red chili flakes.
Add sliced shallots, mint, cilantro, and green onions. Toss to combine.
Spoon mixture into butter lettuce cups and serve immediately.

title: Mediterranean Salmon Power Bowl
description: Pan-seared salmon over cauliflower rice with olives, cucumber, tomato, and lemon-herb vinaigrette.
instructions: Season salmon fillets with salt, pepper, and half the oregano.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sear salmon skin-side up for 4 minutes, flip, and cook 3-4 more minutes.
In another pan, sauté cauliflower rice with 1 tbsp olive oil for 4-5 minutes until tender.
Whisk remaining olive oil, lemon juice, remaining oregano, salt, and pepper for the vinaigrette.
Divide cauliflower rice into bowls. Top with cucumber, tomatoes, olives, and red onion.
Place salmon on top and drizzle with vinaigrette.

title: Korean Beef Lettuce Wraps
description: Sweet and savory ground beef with coconut aminos and sesame, wrapped in crispy romaine leaves with pickled vegetables.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add ground beef, breaking into small pieces, and cook until browned, about 8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Stir in coconut aminos and rice vinegar. Cook 2 more minutes.
Separate romaine leaves into cups. Fill with beef mixture.
Top with julienned carrots, green onions, and sesame seeds.

title: Mexican Chicken Avocado Soup
description: A warming broth-based soup loaded with shredded chicken, fire-roasted tomatoes, and topped with creamy avocado and cilantro.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté garlic and jalapeño for 1 minute.
Add chicken broth, tomatoes, cumin, and salt. Bring to a boil.
Add chicken breasts whole. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 18-20 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken, shred with two forks, and return to pot.
Stir in lime juice. Ladle into bowls and top with diced avocado and cilantro.

title: Tuna Niçoise Salad
description: Classic French composed salad with seared ahi tuna, green beans, hard-boiled eggs, olives, and Dijon vinaigrette.
instructions: Blanch green beans in salted boiling water for 3 minutes, then shock in ice water. Drain.
Season tuna steaks with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet with 1 tbsp olive oil for 1-2 minutes per side for rare. Slice against the grain.
Whisk remaining olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper for vinaigrette.
Arrange mixed greens on plates. Top with green beans, halved eggs, cherry tomatoes, and olives.
Place sliced tuna on top and drizzle with vinaigrette.

title: Turkey Taco Stuffed Sweet Potatoes
description: Baked sweet potatoes loaded with seasoned ground turkey, black beans, and fresh toppings for a filling lunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Pierce sweet potatoes with a fork and bake for 45-50 minutes until tender.
While potatoes bake, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook ground turkey, breaking apart, until browned.
Add chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt. Stir in black beans and cook 3 minutes.
Split sweet potatoes open and fluff insides with a fork.
Spoon turkey and bean mixture over potatoes. Top with avocado slices and salsa.

title: Lemon Herb Chicken Salad
description: Tender poached chicken mixed with crunchy celery, fresh dill, and a bright lemon-olive oil dressing over greens.
instructions: Place chicken breasts in a pot of salted water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 12-15 minutes until cooked through.
Remove chicken and let cool slightly, then shred or dice.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Add chicken, celery, red onion, dill, and parsley. Toss to coat.
Serve over mixed greens.

title: Vietnamese Beef Pho with Zucchini Noodles
description: Fragrant star anise and ginger beef broth poured over zucchini noodles with thinly sliced beef and fresh herbs.
instructions: Bring bone broth to a simmer with star anise, cinnamon stick, and sliced ginger. Simmer 20 minutes to infuse.
Strain broth and return to pot. Add fish sauce.
Divide spiralized zucchini among four bowls.
Bring broth to a rolling boil. Lay thin beef slices over the zucchini noodles.
Pour boiling broth over the beef to cook it instantly.
Top with bean sprouts, basil, jalapeño slices, and lime wedges.

title: Chimichurri Steak Salad
description: Grilled flank steak sliced over a bed of arugula with roasted sweet potato, red onion, and bright chimichurri dressing.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss diced sweet potato with 1 tbsp olive oil and salt. Roast 20 minutes until tender.
Season flank steak with salt and pepper. Grill or pan-sear over high heat 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
Make chimichurri by combining parsley, oregano, garlic, red wine vinegar, remaining olive oil, red pepper flakes, and a pinch of salt.
Arrange arugula on plates. Top with roasted sweet potato, sliced steak, and red onion.
Drizzle generously with chimichurri.

title: Creamy Coconut Chicken Vegetable Soup
description: A rich and comforting soup with tender chicken, sweet potatoes, kale, and coconut milk, seasoned with turmeric and ginger.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion 4 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, ginger, and turmeric. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced chicken and cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in chicken broth and coconut milk. Add sweet potato cubes.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer 15-18 minutes until sweet potatoes are tender.
Stir in kale and cook 3-4 more minutes until wilted. Season with salt.

title: Italian Sausage and Peppers Bowl
description: Sliced Italian chicken sausage with roasted bell peppers and onions over a bed of sautéed spinach.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss sliced peppers and onion with 1 tbsp olive oil, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper on a sheet pan.
Nestle sausage slices among the vegetables. Roast 20-25 minutes until peppers are tender and sausage is browned.
Meanwhile, heat remaining olive oil in a skillet. Sauté garlic 30 seconds, add spinach and cook until wilted.
Divide spinach among bowls. Top with roasted sausage, peppers, and onions.
Drizzle with balsamic vinegar.

title: Egg Roll in a Bowl
description: All the flavors of an egg roll without the wrapper -- ground turkey, cabbage, carrots, and sesame ginger sauce.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add ground turkey, breaking into crumbles, and cook until browned, about 7 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds.
Add coleslaw mix and stir-fry 4-5 minutes until cabbage is wilted but still has some crunch.
Stir in coconut aminos, sesame oil, and rice vinegar. Toss to coat.
Serve topped with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Lamb Shawarma Bowl
description: Spiced roasted lamb slices over cauliflower tabbouleh with tahini sauce, pickled onions, and cucumber.
instructions: Mix cumin, coriander, turmeric, cinnamon, and half the salt. Rub into lamb slices.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook lamb slices 2-3 minutes per side until browned. Set aside.
Sauté cauliflower rice with remaining olive oil for 4-5 minutes. Let cool slightly.
Toss cauliflower rice with diced cucumber, tomatoes, parsley, and 1 tbsp lemon juice for tabbouleh.
Whisk tahini with remaining lemon juice, 2 tbsp water, and remaining salt until smooth.
Assemble bowls with cauliflower tabbouleh, sliced lamb, and drizzle of tahini sauce.

title: Buffalo Chicken Collard Wraps
description: Shredded buffalo chicken with crunchy celery and avocado ranch, wrapped in sturdy collard green leaves.
instructions: Poach chicken breasts in simmering salted water for 15-18 minutes until cooked through. Shred with two forks.
Toss shredded chicken with hot sauce.
Blend avocado, coconut cream, lemon juice, garlic powder, dill, and salt to make avocado ranch.
Blanch collard leaves in boiling water for 30 seconds, then shock in ice water to make them pliable.
Lay out collard leaves. Spread avocado ranch down the center, then add buffalo chicken, celery, and carrots.
Roll tightly like a burrito, tucking in the sides. Slice in half to serve.

title: Greek Turkey Meatball Soup
description: Tender herb-seasoned turkey meatballs in a light lemony broth with spinach and diced zucchini.
instructions: Mix ground turkey with almond flour, egg, half the garlic, 1 tsp oregano, half the salt, and pepper. Form into 1-inch meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Brown meatballs in batches, about 3 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté remaining garlic 30 seconds. Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Add diced zucchini and remaining oregano. Simmer 8 minutes.
Return meatballs to pot and simmer 10 minutes until cooked through.
Stir in spinach and lemon juice. Cook 2 minutes until spinach wilts. Season with remaining salt.

title: Asian Chicken Cabbage Crunch Salad
description: Shredded chicken over crunchy napa cabbage with edamame, mandarin segments, and a toasted sesame vinaigrette.
instructions: Whisk together sesame oil, rice vinegar, coconut aminos, and grated ginger for the dressing.
In a large bowl, combine sliced napa cabbage, shredded chicken, edamame, mandarin segments, and green onions.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss to coat.
Top with toasted almonds and sesame seeds before serving.

title: Tuscan White Bean and Sausage Soup
description: Hearty Italian-inspired soup with chicken sausage, cannellini beans, tomatoes, and lacinato kale in a savory broth.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Brown sausage slices 3-4 minutes, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté onion 4 minutes. Add garlic and Italian seasoning, cook 1 minute.
Add chicken broth, diced tomatoes, and cannellini beans. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, add sausage back, and simmer 15 minutes.
Stir in kale and cook 5 more minutes until wilted. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Grilled Cod with Mango Avocado Salsa
description: Light and flaky grilled cod topped with a bright mango avocado salsa over mixed greens.
instructions: Combine diced mango, avocado, red bell pepper, red onion, cilantro, and lime juice for the salsa. Set aside.
Brush cod fillets with olive oil and season with chili powder and salt.
Grill or pan-sear cod over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes per side until it flakes easily.
Arrange mixed greens on plates, place cod on top, and spoon mango avocado salsa over each fillet.

title: Curried Butternut Squash Soup with Chicken
description: Velvety roasted butternut squash soup with curry spices and shredded chicken, finished with coconut cream.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss butternut squash with 1 tbsp olive oil and salt. Roast 25-30 minutes until tender.
Heat remaining olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Sauté onion 4 minutes, add garlic, curry powder, and cumin. Cook 1 minute.
Add roasted squash and chicken broth. Bring to a simmer.
Use an immersion blender to puree until smooth.
Stir in coconut cream and shredded chicken. Heat through for 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.

title: BLT Turkey Lettuce Wraps
description: Crispy turkey bacon, fresh tomato, and avocado mayo wrapped in large bibb lettuce leaves for a classic reimagined.
instructions: Cook turkey bacon in a skillet over medium heat until crispy, about 4-5 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
Mash avocado with olive oil mayo, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to make avocado spread.
Separate bibb lettuce into large cup-shaped leaves.
Spread avocado mayo on each leaf. Layer with deli turkey, turkey bacon, and tomato slices.
Roll or fold and serve.

title: Southwest Chicken Power Bowl
description: Grilled chicken with black beans, corn, peppers, and avocado over cauliflower rice with a zesty lime dressing.
instructions: Season chicken with chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt. Grill or pan-sear over medium-high heat 5-6 minutes per side. Slice.
Sauté cauliflower rice with 1 tbsp olive oil for 4-5 minutes.
Whisk remaining olive oil with lime juice for dressing.
Divide cauliflower rice among bowls. Top with sliced chicken, black beans, corn, bell pepper, and avocado.
Drizzle with lime dressing and garnish with cilantro.

title: Japanese Ginger Beef Donburi Bowl
description: Thinly sliced beef simmered in a ginger-coconut aminos sauce, served over steamed rice with quick-pickled cucumbers.
instructions: Toss sliced cucumber with 1 tbsp rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside to quick-pickle.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over high heat. Sear beef slices 1-2 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, cook onion 3-4 minutes. Add ginger, cook 30 seconds.
Add coconut aminos and remaining rice vinegar. Simmer 2 minutes.
Return beef to the skillet and toss to coat.
Serve beef and sauce over rice. Top with pickled cucumber, green onions, and sesame seeds.

title: Moroccan Spiced Chicken Salad
description: Warm cumin and cinnamon-spiced chicken over a bed of roasted root vegetables, dried cranberries, and arugula.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss diced carrots and beets with 1 tbsp olive oil and salt. Roast 20-25 minutes.
Season chicken thighs with cumin, cinnamon, smoked paprika, and salt.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet. Cook chicken 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through. Slice.
Whisk remaining olive oil, lemon juice, and honey for dressing.
Arrange arugula on plates. Top with roasted vegetables, sliced chicken, cranberries, and pumpkin seeds.
Drizzle with dressing.

title: Thai Coconut Chicken Zoodle Bowl
description: Sliced chicken in a creamy red curry coconut sauce over zucchini noodles with fresh basil and crushed peanuts.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken slices 3-4 minutes until browned.
Add red curry paste and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk and fish sauce. Simmer 5 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
Add spiralized zucchini and toss for 2 minutes until just warmed through (don't overcook).
Stir in lime juice. Serve topped with fresh basil and crushed peanuts.

title: Roasted Chicken Harvest Bowl
description: Roasted chicken with Brussels sprouts, sweet potato, dried cranberries, and a maple-Dijon vinaigrette.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss sweet potato and Brussels sprouts with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a sheet pan.
Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper and place on the sheet pan.
Roast 25-30 minutes until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are caramelized.
Whisk remaining olive oil, maple syrup, Dijon mustard, and apple cider vinegar for the dressing.
Slice chicken. Divide vegetables and chicken among bowls. Top with cranberries and pecans.
Drizzle with maple-Dijon vinaigrette.

title: Smoked Salmon Avocado Plate
description: A deconstructed lunch plate with smoked salmon, sliced avocado, capers, hard-boiled eggs, and everything seasoning.
instructions: Arrange smoked salmon, avocado slices, hard-boiled egg halves, cucumber slices, and red onion on two plates.
Scatter capers over the salmon.
Drizzle olive oil over everything. Sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and fresh dill.
Serve with lemon wedges for squeezing.

title: Chicken Tortilla-Less Soup
description: A bold and smoky chicken soup with fire-roasted tomatoes, peppers, and topped with crispy plantain strips.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in a pot. Sauté onion, poblano, and garlic 4 minutes.
Add cumin, chili powder, and smoked paprika. Stir 30 seconds.
Add broth, tomatoes, and salt. Bring to a boil. Add chicken breasts, reduce heat, cover, and simmer 18-20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat remaining avocado oil in a skillet. Fry plantain slices until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Remove chicken, shred, and return to soup. Stir in lime juice.
Serve topped with crispy plantain strips.

title: Taco-Spiced Beef Cauliflower Rice Bowl
description: Seasoned ground beef over cilantro-lime cauliflower rice with pico de gallo, guacamole, and hot sauce.
instructions: Cook ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking apart, until browned. Drain excess fat.
Season beef with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, and half the salt.
Sauté cauliflower rice with olive oil, half the cilantro, 1 tbsp lime juice, and remaining salt for 4-5 minutes.
Make pico de gallo: combine diced tomato, red onion, remaining cilantro, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
Mash avocados with remaining lime juice and a pinch of salt for guacamole.
Assemble bowls: cauliflower rice, taco beef, pico de gallo, and guacamole. Add hot sauce if desired.

title: Ethiopian Berbere Chicken Salad
description: Warm berbere-spiced chicken over a cool salad of lentils, tomatoes, and red onion with a lemon dressing.
instructions: Mix smoked paprika, cayenne, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and half the salt for berbere spice blend.
Coat chicken breasts with 1 tbsp olive oil and the spice blend.
Cook chicken in a skillet over medium heat, 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Toss cooked lentils with cherry tomatoes, red onion, and parsley.
Whisk remaining olive oil, lemon juice, and remaining salt for dressing. Pour over lentil salad.
Serve sliced chicken over the lentil salad.

title: Deconstructed Salmon Sushi Bowl
description: Seared salmon over sushi rice with cucumber, avocado, pickled ginger, nori strips, and coconut aminos.
instructions: Season salmon with salt. Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sear salmon 3-4 minutes per side.
Stir rice vinegar into warm sushi rice.
Divide rice among bowls. Arrange sliced cucumber and avocado alongside.
Place seared salmon on top. Garnish with nori strips, pickled ginger, and sesame seeds.
Drizzle with coconut aminos.

title: Zucchini Beef Chili
description: A hearty no-bean chili loaded with ground beef, zucchini, tomatoes, and warm spices, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown ground beef, breaking apart, for 8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add onion, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook 4 minutes until softened.
Add chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika. Stir 30 seconds.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and salt. Bring to a simmer.
Add diced zucchini. Cover and simmer 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Adjust seasoning and serve.

title: Caribbean Jerk Turkey Rice Bowl
description: Jerk-spiced turkey thighs over coconut rice with mango salsa and sautéed collard greens.
instructions: Cook jasmine rice with coconut milk and enough water per package directions. Fluff when done.
Mix allspice, thyme, cayenne, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt. Rub onto turkey thighs.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook turkey 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through. Slice.
Sauté collard greens with remaining olive oil and a pinch of salt for 4-5 minutes until tender.
Make mango salsa: toss diced mango with lime juice.
Assemble bowls: coconut rice, sliced jerk turkey, collard greens, and mango salsa.

title: Italian Antipasto Salad
description: A loaded Italian salad with salami, marinated artichoke hearts, roasted peppers, olives, and red wine vinaigrette.
instructions: Whisk olive oil, red wine vinegar, Italian seasoning, and salt for the dressing.
Arrange chopped romaine on a large platter or in individual bowls.
Top with salami, artichoke hearts, roasted red peppers, olives, red onion, tomatoes, and pepperoncini.
Drizzle dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
